specific_location: Approximately 1/2 mile west of I-25 on 310 Road (Exit 42). The bird was on the north side of the road about 200' west of RR tracks. It was low on the hillside, below eye level.
description: Size was slightly smaller than a junco. Distinctive medium gray (head, nape, breast and belly). Back reddish with black streaks. Bill small and pale. No white tail flash apparent when the bird flew. No black chin spot.Behaviors: The bird appeared to be foraging in xeric habitat on a south-facing slope.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
